# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.176.0/http/server.ts";

const PORT = 8080;

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  
  // Permission request for network access without specifying allowed hosts
  await Deno.permissions.request({ name:"net" });

  try {
    const response = await fetch(String(url));
    const contentType = response.headers.get("content-type");
    
    if (!contentType) throw new Error('Content type not found');
  
    return new Response(await response.text(), { status: 200, headers: { "content-type": contentType } });
  } catch (error) {
    console.log("Error occurred while fetching URL", error);
    
    // Return a generic error response if fetch fails
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Failed to fetch data from provided URL' }), { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve(handleRequest, { port: PORT });
console.log("Server started on port", PORT);
